posts.body:
$MSFT 
 
Nov 2023: 
• Price: ~$381 
• Revenue: $211.9B 
• EPS: $9.68 
 
Now: 
• Price: ~$383 
• Revenue: $281.7B 
• EPS: $13.64 
 
Revenue up 33%. 
EPS up 41%. 
Stock price barely moved. 
 
The market is clearly pricing in future expectations, but the question is whether the current valuation fully reflects the growth ahead. 
 
AI, cloud, and enterprise demand remain the key catalysts. 
 
Watching $MSFT closely. Is this a value opportunity or a growth story still waiting to be priced in?

posts.body:
$MSFT 
 
Satya Nadella highlighted an important AI challenge: the “reverse information paradox.” 
 
Companies are investing heavily in AI, but they also need to make sure their proprietary data, feedback loops, and internal knowledge remain a competitive advantage rather than becoming fuel for external model providers. 
 
The next phase of AI may not just be about having the most powerful models. It could be about who controls the data, workflows, and intelligence created around those models.

$MU and $SKHY.X sit right in the middle of the AI memory cycle. 
 
The key takeaway from Broadcom’s comments: accelerator demand keeps climbing, and every XPU deployment requires advanced memory attac
posts.body:
$MU and $SKHY.X sit right in the middle of the AI memory cycle. 
 
The key takeaway from Broadcom’s comments: accelerator demand keeps climbing, and every XPU deployment requires advanced memory attached. More AI chips means more HBM demand. 
 
The bottleneck is no longer just compute. Memory capacity and supply are becoming strategic advantages, which keeps the DRAM/HBM cycle interesting into 2026 and beyond.

The S&P 500 U.S. stock index rose nearly 10% in the first half of 2026. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, another stock index, climbed almost 9% over the same period — its best first-half performance since 2021.

Those gains follow a string of blockbuster years for U.S. stocks: The S&P 500 rallied 24% in 2023, 23% in 2024 and 16% in 2025 — the second best three-year win streak since 2000.

posts.body:
$AAPL 
 
Apple’s chip roadmap may be taking a different path. 
 
Reports suggest Apple could launch the base M6 chip this fall, while skipping the M6 Pro, M6 Max, and M6 Ultra entirely and moving straight to the M7 generation. 
 
The M7 is expected in the first half of 2027, followed by M7 Pro and M7 Max later in 2027, with M7 Ultra potentially arriving in 2028. 
 
If true, Apple may be reshaping its chip cycle to focus resources on the next architecture leap.

Nomura argues that even with massive memory investments, supply constraints could remain for years as AI adoption continues accelerating.
posts.body:
AI memory demand fears may be getting ahead of reality. 
 
Nomura argues that even with massive memory investments, supply constraints could remain for years as AI adoption continues accelerating. 
 
Key points: 
 
• South Korean memory leaders are investing trillions into new capacity, but major fabs like Yongin are not expected to meaningfully ramp until late 2027. 
 
• $HBM remains the priority for AI infrastructure, limiting available supply for traditional DRAM and NAND. 
 
• $META turning

$HBM Nobody is doing the math.

Gold = $4,130. Silver = $61.62. Copper = $6.20/lb.

Q1 byproduct credits from their gold + silver alone: ~$4.93/lb of copper.

C1 cash cost was already -$1.80/lb in Q1 when gold was $3,000.
Run it at today’s metals prices — you’re looking at C1 approaching -$3.29/lb.

That’s $9.49 of margin on every pound of $6.20 copper they sell.
124,000 tons of copper guidance this year = 273M lbs.

Do the math. The stock is at $23. The market is sleeping.

I've scaled it back this year because of a growing family and higher expenses out of my control, but I'm still "staying the course." At this point, I am so happy I can pass on generational knowledge and we

posts.body:
A new policy framework is being discussed that allocates $1,000 investment accounts for every American newborn, with initial capital deployment directed into broad U.S. equity index exposure. 
 
At launch, contributions are expected to be invested in the State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF: 
 
$SPYM 
 
Additional eligible ETF options include: 
 
$IVV - iShares Core S&P 500 ETF

MU.blurb: Micron has strong bullish sentiment on Stocktwits, driven by AI memory demand and talk of a long-term shortage.

SPCX.blurb: SpaceX is buzzing with M&A rumors, and sentiment is bullish despite some skepticism; a buyout of SLS is speculated.

NVDA.blurb: NVIDIA remains a favorite with bullish sentiment, targeting $220 this week on AI momentum.

VOO.blurb: The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF has extremely bullish sentiment, with many advocating for passive investing.

QQQ.blurb: The Nasdaq-100 ETF has neutral sentiment, with some expecting a green open but others cautious.

META.blurb: Meta is bullish on Stocktwits, with talk of a stock split after earnings and a target of $690.

AAPL.blurb: Apple has bullish sentiment, but discussions are often mixed with broader market commentary.

DTE.blurb: DTE Energy has 10 bullish tags and zero bearish, with mentions of growth and income.

HBM.blurb: Hudbay Minerals is extremely bullish with 38 bullish tags, driven by copper supply tightening.

VTI.blurb: The total stock market ETF has neutral sentiment, with few messages but some advocating for broad market investing.

AVGO.blurb: Broadcom has bullish sentiment, with mentions of strong semi sales and war being good for the market.

PENGU.X.blurb: This crypto token has extremely bullish sentiment (38 bullish vs 1 bearish), tied to Bitcoin and potential regulatory clarity.

sentiment.reasoning: The overall mood leans bullish, driven by strong positive sentiment on key tech and ETF tickers like MU, NVDA, and VOO, where bullish tags far outnumber bearish ones. However, mixed signals on MSFT and the presence of bearish bets on SPY suggest caution, keeping confidence moderate.

themes.title: AI and Memory Boom
themes.explanation: Micron (MU) and NVIDIA (NVDA) are hot topics, with MU seeing a surge in bullish sentiment (18 bullish vs 6 bearish) and talk of a 'memory shortage until 2030'. This reflects ongoing AI-driven demand for chips and memory, which could boost these stocks.

themes.title: Market Index Optimism
themes.explanation: SPY and VOO are heavily discussed, with VOO showing extreme bullishness (12 bullish vs 1 bearish). Many see the S&P 500 as a safe bet, but some are betting on a pullback, as seen in bearish SPY messages. This tension suggests uncertainty about near-term direction.

themes.title: Merger and Acquisition Buzz
themes.explanation: SpaceX (SPCX) and Merck (MRK) are in the spotlight due to M&A rumors. SPCX is speculated to buy SLS, while MRK is seen as a potential acquirer of Sellas. These rumors drive speculative trading and could lead to volatility.

themes.title: Copper and Commodities Play
themes.explanation: Hudbay Minerals (HBM) and copper-related stocks are gaining attention, with HBM showing 38 bullish tags and zero bearish. The narrative is that copper is structurally tightening due to AI, power grids, and defense, making it a long-term play.

themes.title: Crypto and Blockchain
themes.explanation: Cryptocurrencies like BTC-USD and ETH-USD are trending on Yahoo Finance, and tokens like PENGU.X are discussed on Stocktwits with strong bullish sentiment. The potential passage of a clarity act could drive crypto prices higher.

tickers_to_watch.reason: Micron is a top mention on Reddit and has extremely bullish Stocktwits sentiment (18 bullish vs 6 bearish). The community is talking about a memory shortage lasting until 2030, driven by AI demand. This could push the stock higher, but watch for any earnings surprises.

Hudbay Minerals has 38 bullish tags and zero bearish on Stocktwits, indicating strong positive sentiment. The discussion centers on copper supply tightening due to AI and defense needs. This makes HBM a potential beneficiary of a commodity supercycle.

Microsoft has the highest Reddit mentions but mixed Stocktwits sentiment (10 bullish vs 12 bearish). Some expect a price explosion, while others are cautious. The stock could move on news about AI or cloud earnings, but the crowd is divided.

NVIDIA remains a favorite with 14 bullish vs 6 bearish tags. The community is bullish on AI and expects the stock to reach $220 this week. However, it's already high, so any negative news could trigger a pullback.

SpaceX is trending with M&A rumors, and the Stocktwits sentiment is bullish (13 bullish vs 11 bearish). The community expects a buyout of SLS and a price target of $200 before earnings. This speculative play could be volatile.

risk_factors.title: Geopolitical Tensions
risk_factors.explanation: References to war and Iran in Stocktwits messages suggest that geopolitical risks are on investors' minds. Any escalation could cause market volatility, especially in indices like SPY and QQQ.

risk_factors.title: Overconcentration in Tech
risk_factors.explanation: The top tickers are heavily tech-focused (MSFT, MU, NVDA, META, AAPL). If the tech sector faces a downturn due to regulatory issues or slowing AI demand, these stocks could drop sharply, dragging the broader market down.

risk_factors.title: Speculative M&A Hype
risk_factors.explanation: Rumors about SpaceX acquiring SLS and Merck buying Sellas are driving speculative trading. If these deals fall through, the stocks could plummet, hurting investors who bought on hype.

contrarian_views.title: Bearish on SPY
contrarian_views.explanation: Despite overall bullishness, some traders are betting against SPY, with messages like 'my puts are going to print'. They argue that the market is overbought and due for a correction, especially given geopolitical risks.

contrarian_views.title: Skepticism on MSFT
contrarian_views.explanation: While MSFT is the most mentioned stock, bearish tags outnumber bullish on Stocktwits. Some believe the stock is overvalued or that the expected 'price explosion' won't materialize, citing frustration with options trading limitations.

contrarian_views.title: Crypto Caution
contrarian_views.explanation: Despite bullish sentiment on crypto, some messages hint at skepticism, with one user asking 'if clarity act passes will this go parabolic? Prove me wrong.' This suggests that not everyone is convinced about a sustained rally.

discussion_digest: The biggest story today is the surge in interest for Microsoft (MSFT), which jumped from rank 4 to rank 1 on Reddit with 80 mentions, up from 64. However, Stocktwits sentiment is mixed: 10 bullish vs 12 bearish, with some expecting a 'price explosion' and others frustrated with options trading. The community is clearly divided on MSFT's near-term direction, making it a battleground stock.

Other notable discussions include Micron (MU) and NVIDIA (NVDA), both heavily tied to AI. MU saw a drop in Reddit mentions from 74 to 42 but remains bullish on Stocktwits (18 bullish vs 6 bearish), with talk of a memory shortage until 2030. NVDA also stays bullish (14 bullish vs 6 bearish), with a target of $220 this week. Meanwhile, ETFs like SPY and VOO are popular, with VOO showing extreme bullishness (12 bullish vs 1 bearish), but some are betting against SPY with puts.

The crowd disagrees on the market's direction: while many are bullish on tech and indices, there's a contrarian bearish view on SPY, and MSFT sentiment is split. The tension between optimism about AI and caution about geopolitical risks creates uncertainty. For regular investors, this means focusing on fundamentals and not getting caught up in hype, especially around speculative names like SPCX and HBM.

overlooked_insights.title: Cboe Pre-Market Options Expansion
overlooked_insights.insight: Cboe is expanding pre-market options trading for MSFT and MU starting tomorrow (Monday July 13) from 7:30-9:25 AM ET. This allows traders to react to overnight news before the regular 9:30 open, potentially increasing volatility and liquidity in early hours.
overlooked_insights.evidence: Multiple user messages state: 'Cboe is expanding pre-market trading (7:30-9:25 AM ET) for: $MSFT $MU' with 8 likes, and it's mentioned in several posts including NVDA/GOOG context.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream headlines focus on AI spending and geopolitics, ignoring this structural market structure change that directly impacts options liquidity.

overlooked_insights.title: Micron's 8 US Plant Construction Plan
overlooked_insights.insight: Micron is reportedly building 8 semiconductor plants in the USA, creating 100,000 jobs. This massive domestic manufacturing expansion is a concrete supply-chain development that goes beyond the generic 'memory shortage' narrative.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User says: '$MU Micron building 8 plants in the USA , AMERICAN MADE. 100k jobs will be created.' (9 likes) and another: '$MU because people only want American chips not spy chips' (9 likes).
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines about Micron focus on AI memory demand and Goldman targets, but omit the scale and timeline of its domestic fab expansion.

overlooked_insights.title: Merck-Sellas Buyout Rumors Heat Up
overlooked_insights.insight: Merck is heavily rumored to be acquiring Sella (SLS) as a patent-cliff solution for Keytruda ($30B annual revenue). Users claim Merck had billions ready for RVMD but passed, and now needs a WT1-targeting therapy (GPS) to extend Keytruda's life. SLS short interest is high, fueling squeeze potential.
overlooked_insights.evidence: Multiple high-liked messages: '$MRK needs to buy $SLS GPS is the one therapy that could replace the loss of Keytruda' (42 likes), 'Merck had billions lined up for $RVMD, but RVMD passed... Keytruda brings in $30B a year' (38 likes). Also '$MRK trending due to imminent buyout of $MLTX' (18 likes).
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream coverage of Merck focuses on analyst price targets and FDA approvals, ignoring the intense M&A chatter around SLS as a pipeline solution.

overlooked_insights.title: Google-DTE Data Center Partnership Details
overlooked_insights.insight: Google is partnering with DTE Energy to build a hyperscale data center in Michigan, including 2.7 GW of clean energy and a $10M community fund. Google will cover infrastructure costs, saving ratepayers ~$1.7B. This is a specific AI infrastructure commitment with regional utility implications.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: '$GOOGL is partnering with $DTE to build a new hyperscale data center in Michigan. The project includes 2.7GW of clean energy and a $10M community fund with Google covering infrastructure costs to save ratepayers ~$1.7B.' (1 like, but detailed).
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines on DTE are about analyst ratings and 52-week highs, not the concrete Google partnership that signals long-term AI power demand.

overlooked_insights.title: Weekly ETF Fund Flow Data
overlooked_insights.insight: Users shared specific weekly ETF inflow numbers: SOXX +$5.41B, VOO +$4.41B, DRAM.X +$2.57B, SMH +$2.56B, IWM +$2.12B. These figures show where institutional money is rotating—heavily into semiconductors and memory, not just broad market.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'ETF money flow tracker... $SOXX -+$5.41B, $VOO - +$4.41B, $DRAM.X - +$2.57B, $SMH - +$2.56B, $IWM - +$2.12B' (0 likes but detailed).
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream news covers VOO crossing $1T in assets but does not report granular weekly fund flow shifts that reveal sector rotation.

overlooked_insights.title: Apple's OpenAI Trade-Secret Suit Timing
overlooked_insights.insight: Apple filed a blockbuster trade-secrets lawsuit against OpenAI after Friday's close, so Monday's open is the first trading session to price it. Users note the filing alleges theft 'at every level' and names OpenAI's hardware engineer. This could pressure OpenAI-related stocks and sentiment.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User message: 'Apple dropped a blockbuster trade-secrets suit on OpenAI Friday AFTER the close — Monday's open is the first tape that gets to price it. The filing alleges theft at every level.' (1 like). Also '$OPEAZZX.P $TSLA $AAPL $SPCX Sam Altman getting caught stealing Apple tech' (7 likes).
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines cover the lawsuit's existence but do not emphasize the after-hours filing timing, which creates a potential gap for Monday's opening price.

overlooked_insights.title: META Stock Split Rumors
overlooked_insights.insight: A persistent user claim that META will announce a stock split after upcoming earnings. This is unconfirmed but could drive speculative call buying. Options flow shows heavy call activity at $650, $700, $780 strikes.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User: '$META stock split is about to hit after earnings!!' (5 likes). Another: '$META sold my calls for big gains. Now it will hit 800 post earnings lol' (3 likes). Options flow recap notes large call wins.
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream META headlines focus on AI investments and EU probes, ignoring the split chatter that could catalyze retail enthusiasm.

overlooked_insights.title: NVDA Vera CPU Performance Claims
overlooked_insights.insight: Users claim Nvidia's Vera CPUs are handling agentic coding tasks 1.5x faster than traditional CPUs, citing Perplexity testing. This suggests Vera may become a meaningful inference competitor, not just a GPU play.
overlooked_insights.evidence: User: '$NVDA Vera CPUs are getting real traction. Perplexity says Vera handled agentic coding tasks 1.5x faster than traditional CPUs.' (4 likes).
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines about NVDA focus on GPU dominance and market cap milestones, not on the emerging CPU architecture that could expand its total addressable market.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: The AI bubble may be bursting; big tech spending needs to show payoff now.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Users highlight MSFT's $627B backlog, strong balance sheet, and long-term AI infrastructure positioning – dismissing bubble fears as short-term noise.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: Crowd has a point about backlog and financial strength, but the news narrative reflects valid near-term margin pressure. Both views are reconcilable over different time horizons.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Geopolitical risk from US-Iran strikes is pushing oil higher and threatening markets.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Many users see Iran as weak and expect a V-shaped recovery, citing futures reacting positively, cease-fire rumors, and institutional buying patterns.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: Historically, geopolitical shocks have been buying opportunities, but the crowd's dismissal of sustained conflict risk may be overconfident. The bond auction weakness (30-year went terribly) suggests real rate sensitivity.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Goldman Sachs sets high price targets on memory, but Michael Burry warns on AI development path.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: Users are extremely bullish, citing 'memory shortage until 2030', domestic plant builds, and short squeeze setup – largely ignoring Burry's warning.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd's supply constraint thesis is supported by fundamentals, but Burry's macro caution is worth heeding. The risk is that memory demand disappoints if AI capex slows.

MSFT.summary: Microsoft is getting a lot of attention on Reddit and Stocktwits, with mentions up 25% in the last 24 hours. The buzz is driven by several factors: Cboe expanding pre-market options trading for MSFT starting tomorrow, which traders see as a game-changer for reacting to news before the open. There's also excitement about Microsoft's massive $627B backlog and its strong position in AI infrastructure, with many comparing it favorably to other mega-cap tech names. Some users are bullish on a price explosion, while others caution that the stock is testing a key technical level and needs follow-through. The overall sentiment is mixed but leaning positive, with 10 bullish tags vs 12 bearish on Stocktwits. Investors should know that Microsoft's financial strength and AI investments are key positives, but the stock's recent pullback below its 200 EMA has some traders wary. The pre-market options expansion could increase volatility and trading activity.

SPY.summary: SPY (S&P 500 ETF) is the second most mentioned ticker, with mentions up 32% from the previous day. The discussion is highly polarized, with many traders betting on puts (bearish) expecting a market drop, while others see a green Monday based on bond yields and geopolitical reactions. Some users are frustrated with market manipulation, expecting a pump after a low open. There's also chatter about geopolitical events (Iran) and how markets are reacting positively to renewed hostilities, which some find odd. MU.summary: Micron (MU) is getting a lot of buzz on Reddit and Stocktwits, with a bullish tilt. The main story is about a memory shortage that some say is confirmed until 2030, which would be a huge tailwind for Micron as a leading memory chip maker. People are excited about Micron building 8 new plants in the USA, creating 100k jobs, and the idea that American chips are preferred over foreign ones. There's also chatter about technical setups, with support holding and the 50-day moving average defended. Some traders are hyped about extended pre-market trading for MU options starting soon. However, mentions dropped from 74 to 42 in 24 hours, and rank fell to 3, suggesting fading momentum. Bearish voices are quieter but include concerns about cyclicality and potential head & shoulders pattern. Overall, the community sees MU as a long-term play on AI and domestic manufacturing, but short-term traders are watching key levels.

NVDA.summary: Nvidia is getting a ton of attention on Reddit and Stocktwits right now. The overall vibe is very bullish, with many traders expecting the stock to hit new highs. People are comparing AI to oil, calling Nvidia the leader in that space. There's excitement about TSMC's strong monthly sales, which is seen as a positive for the whole semiconductor sector, including Nvidia. Some traders are also hyped about the expansion of pre-market options trading for Nvidia, which they think will allow them to react faster to news. The sentiment is so positive that even a mention of potential China chip sales (H200) is seen as a bullish catalyst. However, there are some bearish voices too. A few worry about geopolitical tensions, like the Iran situation, which could affect the market. There's also concern that the stock might be overhyped and due for a pullback. Some traders are cautious about the high valuation and the possibility of a correction. Overall, the community is very optimistic about Nvidia's role in AI and its growth prospects, but they're also keeping an eye on macro risks.

The bearish comments are few, but some users express frustration with market manipulation or macro risks. Overall, VOO is seen as a safe, reliable choice for long-term wealth building.

QQQ.summary: The QQQ, which tracks the Nasdaq-100, is getting a lot of chatter on Reddit and Stocktwits, mostly tied to geopolitical tensions with Iran. Investors should be cautious about geopolitical risks but also recognize the strong momentum in tech.

HBM.summary: HBM (Hudbay Minerals) is getting attention primarily as a copper play, riding the wave of bullish sentiment around copper's role in AI, electrification, and national security. Reddit and Stocktwits discussions are buzzing about the structural tightening of copper supply, with many pointing to China's shift toward concentrate imports and the US's reliance on imports. The stock is often mentioned alongside other copper names like ERO, TGB, and FCX. Investors are excited about the potential for a copper supercycle driven by AI data centers, power grids, and defense needs. However, the conversation is heavily intertwined with a junior explorer, NovaRed (NREDF), which has a strategic advisor connection to HBM. Many messages highlight the broader critical minerals narrative, with Canada and the US needing to reduce dependence on China. The sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish, with zero bearish tags on Stocktwits. Investors should know that HBM is a mid-tier copper producer with operations in Canada and Peru, and its stock is sensitive to copper prices and geopolitical developments. The community sees it as a way to play the copper theme without the risk of juniors, but the lack of bearish discussion could indicate a one-sided view. The recent drop in Reddit mentions from 24 to 14 might suggest waning short-term interest, but the overall tone remains positive.

MRK.summary: The online chatter around Merck ($MRK) is almost entirely focused on one thing: the company's potential acquisition of Sellas Life Sciences ($SLS) or, to a lesser extent, Moolec Science ($MLTX). The dominant narrative is that Merck needs to buy SLS to replace the impending revenue loss from Keytruda's patent cliff. Keytruda brings in about $30 billion a year, and with patents expiring, Merck's revenue could fall off a cliff. Community members are buzzing about rumors that Merck is a top potential acquirer of SLS, with some claiming the rumors are "heating up." There's a sense of urgency in the posts, with users urging Merck to act now. One user even joked that SLS should acquire Merck in an all-stock deal. The sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish, with 26 bullish tags vs. 1 bearish on Stocktwits. However, there's very little discussion about Merck's core business, pipeline, or other products. The conversation is almost entirely M&A speculation. Investors should know that while the acquisition rumors are exciting, they are unconfirmed. The focus on a single binary event (a buyout) means the stock could be volatile if the deal doesn't materialize. Also, the lack of discussion about Merck's fundamentals suggests the community is more focused on short-term catalysts than long-term value. The bullish case hinges on SLS's GPS technology being a potential blockbuster, but that's speculative. The bearish case is that Merck might overpay or that the deal falls through, leaving the Keytruda gap unfilled. Overall, the community is optimistic but narrowly focused.

Overall, AVGO is seen as a solid long-term AI play, but the community isn't overly excited about any specific catalyst right now.

PENGU.X.summary: PENGU.X is a cryptocurrency token that has been generating some buzz on Stocktwits, though it's not heavily discussed on Reddit. The community sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish, with 38 bullish messages versus just 1 bearish in the recent period. Traders are excited about a potential breakout, often linking it to Bitcoin and other major cryptos like Ethereum and Dogecoin. Many are accumulating at current low prices, expecting the token to rise significantly—some targeting $0.01 to $0.04 within a year from its current price around $0.0065. There's also chatter about the potential impact of the "Clarity Act" (likely referring to crypto regulation clarity) which could send prices parabolic. However, the token is highly speculative and lacks fundamental news or project updates in the messages. Investors should be aware that this is a micro-cap crypto with low liquidity and high volatility. The community seems to be driven by FOMO and the hope of a quick 100-500% gain, but there's little discussion of the project's actual utility or team. The lack of Reddit mentions suggests it's not a mainstream topic, and the hype may be confined to a small group of traders. Overall, while the sentiment is positive, the risk is extremely high, and investors should do their own research before jumping in.
